skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: Generate AI images with GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: GPT-Image-2, FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image, gpt image, openai image, chatgpt image
skill.summary: Generate images with 50+ AI models via the belt CLI from inference.sh
skill.author: halt-catch-fire

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 55 potential issues, but all are false positives after manual review. The 44 'external_commands' findings are bash code examples in markdown documentation blocks (e.g., `belt app run ...`), not actual shell execution. The 10 'network' findings are legitimate documentation URLs pointing to inference.sh, GitHub, and the belt CLI install guide. The 1 'weak cryptographic algorithm' finding at line 3 is the YAML description field containing the word 'bytedance' or similar text fragments — not actual cryptographic code. The skill is a documentation file instructing Claude to use the belt CLI tool, which is a legitimate image generation service.

low_findings.title: External CLI tool invocation (belt CLI)
low_findings.description: The skill instructs Claude to invoke the 'belt' CLI from inference.sh for image generation. This is a legitimate documented tool, not a security risk. The allowed-tools declaration limits execution to 'belt *' commands only.

content.user_title: Generate AI images with 50+ models via Claude
content.value_statement: Claude can generate professional AI images using GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and other leading models through a simple CLI. This skill provides ready-to-use prompt templates and model recommendations for any image generation task.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using 50+ AI models including GPT-Image-2, FLUX, Gemini, and Grok
Edit existing images with natural language instructions (background changes, style transfer)
Upscale images to higher resolutions using the Topaz upscaler
Stitch multiple images together horizontally or vertically
Use LoRA models for custom styles and faster generation
Render text accurately in generated images using Seedream or Reve models
limitations: Requires installation of the belt CLI tool from inference.sh
Requires a free inference.sh account and authentication via 'belt login'
Some models cost credits per generation (pricing varies by model)
Image generation speed depends on the model and server load

use_cases.title: Marketing visual creation
use_cases.description: Generate product photos, social media graphics, and ad visuals for marketing campaigns without hiring a photographer.

use_cases.title: Concept art and illustration
use_cases.description: Create concept art, character designs, and illustrations for games, books, or presentations.

use_cases.title: Rapid prototyping for designers
use_cases.description: Quickly generate visual mockups and design references to explore ideas before final production.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [subject] in [style] with [lighting/mood]. Use the FLUX Dev model.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation for general use

prompt_templates.title: Product photography
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a professional product photo of [product] with studio lighting on a clean white background. Use GPT-Image-2 for high quality.

prompt_templates.title: Image editing with instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the image at [URL] and [editing instruction, e.g., 'change the background to a sunset beach']. Use GPT-Image-2 editing.

prompt_templates.title: Text rendering in images
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a poster that says '[exact text]' in [font style] with [visual theme]. Use Reve or Seedream for accurate text rendering.

output_examples.input: Generate a product photo of sneakers with studio lighting
output: The assistant calls: belt app run openai/gpt-image-2 --input '{"prompt": "professional product photo of sneakers, studio lighting", "quality": "high"}'
The CLI returns a URL to the generated image (e.g., https://cloud.inference.sh/files/...)
Claude displays the image URL and describes the result

output_examples.input: Change the background of my photo to a beach at sunset
output: The assistant calls: belt app run openai/gpt-image-2 --input '{"prompt": "change the background to a beach at sunset", "images": ["https://your-image.jpg"]}'
The CLI processes the edit and returns a new image URL

best_practices: Always run 'belt login' first to authenticate before generating images
Use specific, detailed prompts that describe subject, style, lighting, and composition
Choose the right model for the task: GPT-Image-2 for general quality, FLUX Klein for speed and cost, Seedream for text rendering
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make it nice' — be specific about subject, style, and lighting
Do not request images that violate content policies or depict real people without consent
Do not ignore the 'belt login' step — unauthenticated requests will fail

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: The belt CLI is a command-line tool from inference.sh that lets you run AI apps for image generation, video, and more. You install it with 'npx skills add belt-sh/cli'.

faq.question: Do I need to pay for image generation?
faq.answer: inference.sh offers a free tier with limited credits. Some models cost credits per image (e.g., FLUX Klein at $0.0001/image). Check inference.sh for current pricing.

faq.question: Which model should I use?
faq.answer: Use GPT-Image-2 for general high-quality generation and editing. Use FLUX Dev LoRA for custom styles. Use Seedream or Reve for accurate text rendering. Use FLUX Klein for fast, cheap generation.

faq.question: Can I edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. Use GPT-Image-2 with the 'images' parameter to pass an image URL and describe the edit in the prompt (e.g., 'change background to a beach').

faq.question: Is my data private?
faq.answer: Images are processed by inference.sh servers. Do not upload sensitive or private images. Check inference.sh's privacy policy for details.

faq.question: How do I install this skill?
faq.answer: Run 'npx skills add inference-sh/skills@infsh-cli' for the full platform, or use the specific model skills listed in the Related Skills section.
